frameworks.name: artificial neural network with oscillatory dynamics
frameworks.one_line: ANN augmented with alpha-like pulsed inhibition and unit refraction

frameworks.name: multiplexing via temporal coding
frameworks.one_line: Competing inputs separated into sequential output activations over time

frameworks.name: convolutional neural networks from visual cortex
frameworks.one_line: CNN heritage from simple/complex cells motivates the model

frameworks.name: neocortical oscillatory dynamics
frameworks.one_line: Alpha-band rhythmic inhibition imported from cortical models

core_question: Can oscillatory dynamics added to an ANN convert simultaneous competing inputs into a temporal code?
key_assumptions: Refractory hidden units approximate biological neuronal refraction
Pulsed inhibition meaningfully mimics neuronal alpha oscillations
Simultaneous-letter presentation is a valid bottleneck test
manipulations: Trained ANN on individually presented letters
Added refraction to hidden units post-training
Introduced pulsed inhibition mimicking alpha oscillations
Presented single vs two simultaneous letters
key_findings: Without dynamics the network produced mixed output for two simultaneous letters
With refraction and oscillatory inhibition output nodes activated sequentially
Single-letter classification was preserved by the trained network
interpretations: Oscillatory dynamics resolve a bottleneck by multiplexing competing inputs into a temporal code
Combining computational neuroscience and machine learning can implement biologically motivated mechanisms in ANNs
